title: 2007-2021 Toyota Tundra Water Pump Replacement Instructions - Also For Sequoia, Land Cruiser, LX570
description: Replace the water pump on a Toyota Tundra, covering coolant drain, fan and shroud removal, thermostat housing removal, pump swap, and cooling system bleeding.
tips: Spray penetrating oil where the fan mates to the pulley and let it soak before prying; rotate the fan 90° and pry gradually in circles until it breaks free.
Once a gap forms between the fan flange and pulley, insert a screwdriver or pry bar directly into the gap to separate them.

Clean the water pump mating surface with a green Scotch-Brite pad and brake cleaner or water — never use an abrasive disc on a grinder, as it can gouge the aluminum and prevent the gasket from sealing.
Blow out all bolt holes after cleaning so no coolant or water remains inside them.
All water pump bolts go into blind holes — do not apply silicone to any bolt threads, even if old silicone residue is present.
The longer 12mm head bolt goes in a specific hole; confirm placement before torquing.
Use silicone-based grease to hold the O-ring in its groove during thermostat housing installation.
Disconnect the front coolant hose from the engine side rather than at the thermostat housing — it's easier to access.
Fill the cooling system slowly to help it self-bleed and avoid trapping air bubbles.
Bleed the system by gently revving to ~2,000 RPM and watching for bubbles; the system is fully bled when no bubbles appear and the heater blows hot at idle.
If the heater blows hot while revving but cools at idle, air is still trapped — continue bleeding.
